methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Ultima Genomics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Insitro
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.4B

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Cellares, a Series C-stage biotech company founded by Fabian Gerlinghaus and Omar Kurdi, operates in a competitive landscape alongside eight private peers and three public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) in the Biotech & Health sector. The company has raised $350M+ to date.

Two valuation methods anchor this analysis. Stage-progression modeling, using a $12M total-raised proxy and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical for Series C advancement, implies $20M. More substantially, peer-median anchoring across three same-stage, same-sector comparables—Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Insitro ($2.4B), and Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B)—yields a median of $2.4B with a range of $1.5B to $4.0B. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure, typical for private companies. The sector median P/S among public peers is 6.1x.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.2B (median) with bounds of $17M (low) and $2.8B (high), weighted toward the private peer median. Key uncertainties include Cellares' revenue scale relative to named peers, stage-specific capital deployment efficiency, and whether the company commands valuation premium or discount within the peer cohort. The wide spread between stage-progression and peer methods ($20M vs. $2.4B) reflects the extreme early-stage funding proxy; peer data carries greater weight for Series C calibration.
